sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.108 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: The$(G)$of a group$G$can be defined as the normalizer of the subgroup of left translations in the group of all permutations of the set$G$. The$(G)$of$G$is the normalizer of the holomorph. Set$T(G) = (G)/ (G)$. [r]{a)} Is there a centerless group$G$for which$T(G)$is not an elementary abelian 2-group? [r]{b)} Is there a finite centerless group$G$for which$T(G)$is not an elementary abelian 2-group? [...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
